completed_tasks.name: Bootstrap Vite, TypeScript, checks, hooks, CI, and Vercel configuration
completed_tasks.progress: Interrupted implementer created the Vite/React shell, npm tooling, strict TypeScript configuration, ESLint/Prettier/Vitest/Playwright setup, Husky pre-commit hook, GitHub Actions, Dependabot, environment template, and Vercel SPA configuration. npm run check passes, but requirements and E2E coverage are not complete.

completed_tasks.name: Implement the backend-heavy Convex domain
completed_tasks.progress: Draft schema, seed data, catalog query, production query, deterministic recommendations, and hybrid AI action exist. Migrations and rate-limiter components are not installed or configured; the production query does not yet accept the full workflow selection contract; Convex has not been initialized against a deployment.

completed_tasks.name: Create engineering and Claude guidance
completed_tasks.progress: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md drafts exist but are uncommitted and need final review against the completed implementation.

remaining_tasks.name: Finish and verify Convex backend, migrations, and rate limiting
Finalize docs and create the infrastructure commit
Extract typed ephemeral workflow state and Convex client hooks
Rebuild the complete eight-node React interface
Implement responsive editorial CSS and print output
Verify parity, remove legacy runtime, commit, and deploy

blockers.description: The repository is not connected to a Convex development deployment.
blockers.workaround: Resume locally and run npx convex dev; authenticate or select the intended project when prompted.

blockers.description: Vercel AI Gateway credentials are not configured in the Convex environment.
blockers.workaround: Because the action runs on Convex, Vercel deployment OIDC is not automatically available there; configure a scoped AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY in Convex or revise the runtime boundary.

blockers.description: The latest origin/main expanded the parity target after the original plan was written.
blockers.workaround: Treat current UI/ReFleek.dc.html and api/generate.js as the source of truth for pattern upload/generation, render generation, craft constraints, embedded images in PDF export, and failure/loading states.

human_actions_pending.action: Confirm or authenticate the intended Convex project during npx convex dev.
human_actions_pending.context: No CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT or VITE_CONVEX_URL is currently configured.

human_actions_pending.action: Link the repository to the intended Vercel project and enable AI Gateway.
human_actions_pending.context: Production deployment and AI enhancement cannot be verified without project access.

decisions.decision: Use Vite, React, strict TypeScript, and authored CSS.
decisions.rationale: This replaces the generated Design Component runtime with a maintainable SPA while preserving the editorial interface.

decisions.decision: Use a backend-heavy Convex boundary without accounts or workflow persistence.
decisions.rationale: The user selected backend-owned catalogs, production calculations, recommendations, producer matching, impact, and tech-pack output while explicitly declining user persistence.

decisions.decision: Use Vercel AI Gateway only as an enhancement with deterministic fallback.
decisions.rationale: The workflow must remain reliable and demoable when model access, budget, validation, or rate limits fail.

decisions.decision: Preserve the current editorial identity and refine responsiveness.
decisions.rationale: The migration should retain the recognizable ReFleek visual system rather than redesigning it from scratch.

next_action: Read .planning/.continue-here.md, inspect every uncommitted change, then reconcile the draft backend with the current UI parity contract before writing more code.
context_notes: The implementation subagent was explicitly interrupted at the user's request. No application commit or deployment was created. npm run check currently exits 0 with 9 unit tests passing and a successful Vite build, but this is not evidence that the plan is complete: Playwright was not run, Convex codegen/deployment was not run, migrations and distributed rate limiting are missing, and the React workflow has not been built. A late parity audit confirmed the React migration must also preserve categorized and unfinished products, editable dimensions, multi-material patchwork with grid/organic modes, reclaimed-material digital-print blocking, pattern prompt/upload/generation, full repeat and motif layout controls, AI render generation, jsPDF image embedding, mix-aware producer/impact output, and the POST /api/generate request/response contract.
